description: Science activities from the document: Animal Classification Flashcards, Nature Senses Detective, Scientific Mind Map

key_features: Can live on land AND water
Starts life in water with gills
Live in water or on land

classification_quiz.reason: Bats have fur and feed their babies on milk — even though they fly!
Whales breathe air, are warm-blooded, and feed their calves on milk — classic mammal features!
Penguins have feathers, lay eggs, and are warm-blooded — even though they cannot fly!
Spiders have 8 legs and 2 body parts — insects have 6 legs and 3 body parts!
Frogs start life in water as tadpoles with gills, then grow legs and move to land!
Sea horses have gills and fins — they ARE fish, even though they look very different from most!

nature_senses_detective.description: Identify natural items using only ONE sense at a time — builds scientific observation skills

rounds.instruction: Look carefully but do NOT touch, smell, or listen. Describe ONLY what you see.
rounds.buddy_prompt: For each item found: 'Tell me its colour. Tell me its shape. Tell me if it is smooth or rough just from looking. What does it remind you of?'

rounds.instruction: Close your eyes! Feel an item but do NOT look or smell it.
rounds.buddy_prompt: 'Is it warm or cold? Is it hard or soft? Is it heavy or light? Is it smooth or bumpy? Does it have corners? Can you GUESS what it is just from touching?'

rounds.instruction: Smell only — eyes closed, no tasting or touching.
items_to_find: a flower or leaf from outside
a piece of soil or grass
something citrus like lemon or orange peel
rounds.buddy_prompt: 'Is it a strong smell or faint? Is it sweet, sour, earthy, or sharp? Have you smelled this before? Where? Can you NAME the item just from the smell?'

rounds.instruction: Listen very carefully — close your eyes for 30 seconds.
rounds.buddy_prompt: 'What sounds did you hear? Were they high or low? Near or far? Steady or changing? If you heard something you could not identify — what COULD it be?'

mind_map.description: Connecting small ideas to big ideas — Scientific Mind Map for Class 3–5

connection_chain: A seed sprouts when water, warmth and air are available
The seedling grows leaves to capture sunlight
Leaves use sunlight + CO₂ + water to make glucose
This process (photosynthesis) produces oxygen as a byproduct
Animals eat plants and breathe the oxygen
Animals exhale CO₂ — which plants absorb again
This endless exchange is the carbon cycle — life on Earth depends on it!
nodes.wow_fact: Every breath of oxygen you take was made by a plant. You are alive because of photosynthesis!

connection_chain: A magnet pulls iron without touching it
This is a non-contact force — it acts through space
Gravity also acts without touching (Earth pulls the Moon)
Electromagnetic forces hold atoms together
These are two of the four fundamental forces in the universe
Everything that happens in the universe comes down to these four forces!
nodes.wow_fact: Magnetism and electricity are actually two sides of the same force — electromagnetism! Einstein discovered this.

nodes.big_idea: Conservation of energy and the water cycle
connection_chain: Your body heat (thermal energy) transfers to the ice
The ice gains enough energy to break its crystal structure
It melts — a state change from solid to liquid
The water can then evaporate (liquid to gas) if more energy is added
Water vapour rises, cools, condenses into droplets — clouds
Rain falls — completing the water cycle
The total energy in the system is always conserved — it just changes form!
nodes.wow_fact: The water you hold in your hand has been ice, liquid water, and water vapour thousands of times in its multi-billion-year journey!
